mnemonics: GRADIENT DESCENT: Glide Rapidly Along Downhill, In Every Direction, Navigating Towards Steepest Error Minimum
keyPoints: The gradient of MSE points in the direction of the steepest increase in error.
Taking the negative of the gradient gives the direction of steepest descent for minimizing error.
The magnitude of the gradient indicates how steep the error surface is at that point.
commonMistakes: Forgetting the factor of 2 that results from differentiating the squared term (d/dθ (y - ŷ)² = 2(y - ŷ) * dŷ/dθ).
Confusing the gradient (a vector) with its magnitude (a scalar) when updating parameters.
Applying the gradient descent update rule without a learning rate, leading to overshooting the minimum.
